sections.title: Major Diseases & Causes
points: Polio: Caused by a Virus. Spreads via the faecal-oral route. Leads to muscle wasting and paralysis.
Rickets: Caused by lack of Vitamin D/Calcium in children. Results in soft bones that bend (bowlegs).
TB of Spine: Caused by Mycobacterium bacteria. Causes a painful lump/deformity on the backbone.
Tetanus (Lockjaw): Caused by bacteria in soil entering via deep cuts or the umbilical cord of newborns.
Leprosy: A bacterial disease spread by long-term contact. Causes skin patches with loss of feeling.
Scurvy: Caused by lack of Vitamin C. Weakens connective tissues, leading to joint pain and bleeding gums.

sections.title: Prevention & Public Health
points: Boil/Pasteurize Milk: Essential to kill TB bacteria that may come from infected cows.
Proper Latrine Use: Prevents Polio germs from reaching the community water supply.
Wound Care: Cleaning cuts with Antiseptics prevents Tetanus bacteria from growing.

sections.text: Skeletal Nutrition: Vitamin D is the 'key' that lets Calcium into the bones. Without the sun or proper food, bones remain soft even if you drink milk! Osteomalacia is the name for Rickets when it affects adults.

cards.fact: Tetanus in newborns caused by using unsterile tools on the umbilical cord.
cards.mnemonic: Keep the Cord clean to keep Tetanus away!

cards.fact: Thinning of bones in older age (especially women) due to loss of minerals.
The bacterium that causes TB in both lungs and bones.
